title: Sieglingia nealleyi
taxonomy: Sieglingia nealleyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to North America and is also known as a species of dandelion. Sieglingia nealleyi is classified as an annual or perennial herb, with a taproot and a stem that can grow up to 1 meter in height. The plant has leaves that are pinnately divided and a flower head that is surrounded by a ring of bracts. The classification of Sieglingia nealleyi is further supported by its placement in the tribe Cichorieae, a group of plants that are closely related to the dandelions.
